playerClass.name: Fabulist
playerClass.description: Your penchant for writing fables allows you to convert Encounters players may have into Traits that stay with them

playerClass.name: Bard
playerClass.description: Your skill with spreading a good tale allows you to spread story outcomes to all players, including yourself

playerClass.name: Scribe
playerClass.description: Your fondness for recording the histories of other adventurers allows you to add Traits to other players

playerClass.name: Herald
playerClass.description: Your rank allows you to give new titles to other players

location.description: There is a wide river ahead that intersects with the road which becomes a great wooden bridge briefly as it passes over. Various large animals drink lazily from the edges of the water as if they are not typically expecting humans to pass by.
location.label: A Wide Winding River

location.description: The green of the grasses and trees around you slowly gives way ahead to the oranges and purples of great mushroom caps. Bright glowing shelves grow from the sides of the trees ahead and great eerie tube shaped stalks jut like coral up into the sky around them on all sides. You can make out various clumsy structures built into the sides of several of these strange plants.
location.label: A Strange Fungal Forest

location.description: The green of the grasses and trees around you slowly gives way ahead to the oranges and purples of great mushroom caps. Bright glowing shelves grow from the sides of the trees ahead and great eerie tube shaped stalks jut like coral up into the sky around them on all sides. gameSessionDisplay.mapDescription: Every year it comes. The Drop. The adults all gather at the edge of the wood and usher the youngsters into the darkness of the wood at the edge of The Lost Lands. The texts left behind by the Gods tell us that the young people who travel into the wood will encounter the characters, creatures, and spirits who will guide them into adulthood. If they manage to make it through The Drop alive they do so as adults. This year, finally, is our year. We hug our parents, clasp our hands together, and we embark.
gameSessionDisplay.goalDescription: We coalesce once again, but this time we gather at the other end of the wood. The deep forest of The Lost Lands ends abruptly in a sheer cliff that gives way to the mouth of a cave. We enter and gaze into the dark pool of water within. Each of us gains a shadowy glimpse of our future selves.
gameSessionDisplay.playerDescription: We meet up to rest in around a fire in the middle of the eternal night of The Lost Lands. Even after only travelling halfway we have all changed. We discuss one by one who we are becoming.

locations.description: A great stone crest emblazoned with vine-like patterns lies here amidst a field of wheat. At the center of the crest you can make out carefully placed bushels of local crops in various states of rot. There are clearly still worshippers of this old deity in this wood.
locations.label: An Altar to an Old God

locations.description: The sounds of jingling coins and of strange instruments echo from the well lit market on the path ahead. Various colorful shadows hustle and bustle in and out of a great front arch which was clearly assembled just recently.
locations.label: A Grand Night Market

locations.description: The green of the grasses and trees around you slowly gives way ahead to the oranges and purples of great mushroom caps. Bright glowing shelves grow from the sides of the trees ahead and great eerie tube shaped stalks jut like coral up into the sky around them on all sides. You can make out various clumsy structures built into the sides of several of these strange plants.
locations.label: A Strange Fungal Forest

locations.description: There is a wide river ahead that intersects with the road which becomes a great wooden bridge briefly as it passes over. Various large animals drink lazily from the edges of the water as if they are not typically expecting humans to pass by.
locations.label: A Wide Winding River

locations.description: An old farmhouse sits in the middle of a wide open field here. It's difficult to tell from the outside if it is abandoned or not, but there are strange crops here of a kind you have never seen before.
locations.label: An Old Farmhouse

unusedLocations.description: The silence of the wilds is interrupted by the distant sound of a rowdy crowd of tavern patrons. In the distance you can make out the soft glow of roaring fire behind a pair of iron windows. It would be warm and inviting if it wasn't so strange to hear such revelry in the middle of nowhere.
unusedLocations.label: A Bustling Tavern in the Woods

unusedLocations.description: A tower of stones juts haphazardly into the sky ahead. Between the boulders that make up the structure you can make out branches, dead flowers, and even bones as if some unseen force is actively holding it all together.
unusedLocations.label: A Twisting Wizard's Tower

unusedLocations.description: A tall iron structure towers above the trees here. The building appears to be covered in runic markings you do not recognize. You can't make out the language, but you hear the sounds of strange chants from inside.
unusedLocations.label: A Towering Sacred Temple

unusedLocations.description: Your parents warned you that it is very dangerous to travel through the places in the Drop where the thicket grows thick with thorns and the bird sounds grow thin. Which makes it very tempting to find out why.
unusedLocations.label: The Forbidden Wilds

outcomeTypeWithLabel.label: An Altar to an Old God
An Altar to an Old God
